images.title: Constructions récifales d'hermelles (Sabellaria alveolata) (Ifremer 00402-51323 - 35454).jpg
images.description: These worms ( Sabellaria alveolata ), which live in sandy tubes 2 to 4 millimeters in diameter, often group together in masses of 15,000 to 60,000 per square meter, forming gigantic reefs. These can cover areas of several hundred square meters in the tidal swing zone. These hermella reefs have an important ecological role since they support both algae and a variety of fauna.

images.title: Colonisation de récifs d'hermelles par des moules (Ifremer 00402-51326).jpg
images.description: These Sabellaria alveolata worms, which live in sandy tubes measuring 2 to 4 millimeters in diameter, play an important ecological role since they serve as a support for both algae and a variety of fauna. Here, we observe a colonization of hermella reefs ( Sabellaria alveolata ) by mussels.

images.title: Pêche à pied et récifs d'hermelles (Sabellaria alveolata) (Ifremer 00402-51344 - 3131).jpg
images.description: Fishing on foot around reef constructions of hermellas ( Sabellaria alveolata ). These worms which live in sandy tubes of 2 to 4 millimeters in diameter can form gigantic reefs, then serving as support for both algae and a varied fauna.

images.title: Mont-Saint-Michel, dans le brouillard (Ifremer 00402-51332).jpg
images.description: This granite rocky islet, approximately 960 metres in circumference, is located to the east of the mouth of the Couesnon River, in the Manche department in Normandy.
